I've spent all of May trying different social media approaches to just let the world know my novel exists. 

Don't give up! You can do it!
D.L.T.B.G.Y.D.

In the first two days of June, I've made an estimated $23.

What's different?

external.description:
Selon The Information, Meta prévoit aussi de lancer jusqu’à quatre nouveaux modèles de lunettes intelligentes d’ici la fin de l’année, dans le cadre d’une stratégie pour compenser les pertes massives de sa division Reality Labs, spécialisée dans le matériel. Par ailleurs, Meta envisage également d’étendre sa gamme de lunettes connectées au-delà des collaborations avec Ray-Ban et Oakley, avec des modèles comme Modelo prévu pour juin, suivis de Luna, RBM2 Refresh et Mojito VIP d’ici décembre. La société mise sur un service d’abonnement professionnel, Wearables for Work, et vise la vente de 10 millions d’appareils connectés d’ici la seconde moitié de 2026, tout en ciblant au moins 10 entreprises pour équiper leurs équipes. Une stratégie qui pose question puisque Meta semble avoir concéntrer ses effort sur le marché B2C depuis un an.


— Permalink
external.title: Meta Is Reportedly Working On An AI Pendant And More Smart Glasses

external.description:
Anduril a présenté de nouvelles fonctionnalités de ses lunettes EagleEye XR, notamment une vision nocturne à grand champ de vision. Ces lunettes offrent un champ de 84 degrés, une fusion thermique stéréo pour détecter les menaces cachées et un affichage 4K. Elles se distinguent des systèmes conventionnels comme le PVS-31, utilisé par des unités d'élite, par leur intégration des capteurs directement sur le casque, reliés à des lunettes AR dotées de protections balistiques et laser. Le système s'appuie également sur le réseau de surveillance et de défense IA Lattice d'Anduril pour fournir des données en temps réel.

Your website is one of those environments. 
Build it like the AI is already inside.

The brands winning AI search in 2026 understood something early.

You are no longer writing to persuade a human who reads top to bottom. You are writing to be correctly interpreted by an agent that extracts and moves on.

If an AI agent can map a database it has never seen in under two minutes, it can map your page in milliseconds. It will extract whatever is clearest. 

Your only job is to make sure the clearest thing is the thing you want it to say.

東洋思想では世界の理解が根本的に違います。

* 相互依存：万物は関係で存在
* 循環：成長より循環
* 中庸：過剰を避ける
* 無為：自然の流れに従う

ここでは「拡大より調和」が中心概念になります。

Image from pinterest

近代経済学は暗黙のうちに次の前提を置いています。

* 前提A
　人間は欲望を最大化する存在
* 前提B
　社会は生産を拡大することで幸福になる
* 前提C
　自然は資源

record.text:
Your consistently aggressive, defiant, and publicly contemptuous attitude towards me indicates we may not be able to have a productive working relationship. 

No shit.

The gulf between the two is simply too large. With orchestration tools, one "power user" is however many agents they spin up to complete a task, no longer bound by the limits of human frailties. I mean, Netflix could at least assume that one user could only watch 24 hours in a day.

Copilot found themselves in bad spot and had to dig out quickly. Other services have moved more quickly to close loopholes and stem the bleeding. They're all going to land in similar spots, though - in this context it's unsustainable for the mass subscriber base to subsidize the power users.

Of course nobody WANTS to pay for it. There are plenty of services I use that bring value that I gladly pay for, but that doesn't mean I want to. 

It's realistic if it's high enough to cover costs and low enough that people are willing to pay for it.

record.text: The most interesting number isn't the jump in commits. It's the much smaller increase in actual releases. AI can speed up writing code but review, testing and deployment still take time. Are those bottlenecks inefficiencies or safeguards?

The Mandelson/Kyle story isn't just a UK problem. Former officials shaping AI policy while holding industry ties is becoming a familiar pattern. Do any countries have guardrails that are actually strong enough?

Designing for addiction isn't new. Social media already perfected that model. The difference is that AI agents may have access to your email, calendar, files and workflows. That feels like a different level of dependency. Should regulators treat it differently?
